headline: Pandemic Preparedness Budget Cut 42% From COVID-Era Levels
context: Malaysia's pandemic preparedness allocation was reduced from RM1.8 billion in 2023 to RM1.05 billion in 2026.

cards.big: Pandemic preparedness budget cut 42% from RM1.8 billion to RM1.05 billion since 2023
cards.sub: Cuts affect stockpiling surveillance systems and rapid response team readiness

cards.big: National strategic PPE stockpile dropped to 45-day supply from 120-day target level
cards.sub: Budget constraints reduced emergency medical supply reserves below recommended levels

cards.big: WHO warns next pandemic could emerge within 5-10 years based on zoonotic disease patterns
cards.sub: Deforestation and urbanization in Southeast Asia increase spillover event risks

cards.big: South Korea maintains pandemic preparedness at pre-COVID funding levels through dedicated fund
cards.sub: Ring-fenced pandemic budget prevents erosion during normal health budget cycles

cards.big: Cutting pandemic preparedness after a pandemic is like canceling insurance after a car accident
Should pandemic preparedness funding be ring-fenced from annual budget reduction cycles
